segments.text: Yahweh appeared to him by the oaks of Mamre, as he sat in the tent door in the heat of the day.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: He lifted up his eyes and looked, and saw that three men stood near him. When he saw them, he ran to meet them from the tent door, and bowed himself to the earth,
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: and said,
segments.speaker: Abram

segments.text: My lord, if now I have found favor in your sight, please don’t go away from your servant.
segments.speaker: Abraham

segments.text: Now let a little water be fetched, wash your feet, and rest yourselves under the tree.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: I will get a piece of bread so you can refresh your heart. After that you may go your way, now that you have come to your servant.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: They said,
segments.speaker: Yahweh

segments.text: Very well, do as you have said.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Abraham hurried into the tent to Sarah, and said,
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Quickly prepare three seahs[^18] of fine meal, knead it, and make cakes.
segments.speaker: Abraham

segments.text: Abraham ran to the herd, and fetched a tender and good calf, and gave it to the servant. He hurried to dress it.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: He took butter, milk, and the calf which he had dressed, and set it before them. He stood by them under the tree, and they ate.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: They asked him,
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Where is Sarah, your wife?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He said,
There, in the tent.
segments.speaker: Abraham

segments.text: He said,
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: I will certainly return to you at about this time next year; and behold, Sarah your wife will have a son.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Sarah heard in the tent door, which was behind him.
Now Abraham and Sarah were old, well advanced in age. Sarah had passed the age of childbearing.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Sarah laughed within herself, saying,
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: After I have grown old will I have pleasure, my lord being old also?
segments.speaker: Sarah

segments.text: Yahweh said to Abraham,
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Why did Sarah laugh, saying, ‘Will I really bear a child when I am old?’
segments.speaker: Hagar

segments.text: Is anything too hard for Yahweh? At the set time I will return to you, when the season comes around, and Sarah will have a son.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Then Sarah denied it, saying,
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: I didn’t laugh,
segments.speaker: Sarah

segments.text: for she was afraid. He said,
No, but you did laugh.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: The men rose up from there, and looked toward Sodom. Abraham went with them to see them on their way.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Yahweh said,
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Will I hide from Abraham what I do,
segments.speaker: Yahweh

segments.text: since Abraham will surely become a great and mighty nation, and all the nations of the earth will be blessed in him?
segments.speaker: Abraham

segments.text: For I have known him, to the end that he may command his children and his household after him, that they may keep the way of Yahweh, to do righteousness and justice; to the end that Yahweh may bring on Abraham that which he has spoken of him.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Yahweh said,
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Because the cry of Sodom and Gomorrah is great, and because their sin is very grievous,
segments.speaker: Yahweh

segments.text: I will go down now, and see whether their deeds are as bad as the reports which have come to me. If not, I will know.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: The men turned from there, and went toward Sodom, but Abraham stood yet before Yahweh.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Abraham came near, and said,
segments.speaker: Abraham

segments.text: Will you consume the righteous with the wicked?
segments.speaker: Abraham came near, and

segments.text: What if there are fifty righteous within the city? Will you consume and not spare the place for the fifty righteous who are in it?
segments.speaker: Abraham

segments.text: May it be far from you to do things like that, to kill the righteous with the wicked, so that the righteous should be like the wicked. May that be far from you. Shouldn’t the Judge of all the earth do right?
segments.speaker: Abraham

segments.text: Yahweh said,
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: If I find in Sodom fifty righteous within the city, then I will spare the whole place for their sake.
segments.speaker: Yahweh

segments.text: Abraham answered,
segments.speaker: Abraham

segments.text: See now, I have taken it on myself to speak to the Lord, although I am dust and ashes.
segments.speaker: Abraham

segments.text: What if there will lack five of the fifty righteous? Will you destroy all the city for lack of five?
segments.speaker: Abraham

segments.text: He said,
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: I will not destroy it if I find forty-five there.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: He spoke to him yet again, and said,
segments.speaker: Abraham

segments.text: What if there are forty found there?
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: He said,
I will not do it for the forty’s sake.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: He said,
segments.speaker: Abraham

segments.text: Oh don’t let the Lord be angry, and I will speak. What if there are thirty found there?
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: He said,
I will not do it if I find thirty there.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: He said,
segments.speaker: Abraham

segments.text: See now, I have taken it on myself to speak to the Lord. What if there are twenty found there?
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: He said,
I will not destroy it for the twenty’s sake.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: He said,
segments.speaker: Abraham

segments.text: Oh don’t let the Lord be angry, and I will speak just once more. What if ten are found there?
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: He said,
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: I will not destroy it for the ten’s sake.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Yahweh went his way as soon as he had finished communing with Abraham, and Abraham returned to his place.
